evidence_snippets.text: Fees and how to apply Fees differ by length of course and there is a discounted fee for participants from academic institutions and non-profit organisations.
Applicants wishing to apply should complete the online application form.
Essex PG students need to apply via Proficio, in addition to completing the application form .
Participants can refresh and enhance their mathematics knowledge in an intensive course, Mathematics for Social Scientists, which is provided free of charge for participants of the Summer School.
On receipt of the application, the Summer School office will send applicants an invoice for the amount due.
For those participating in online courses, participants must have access to their own computing facilities in the form of a laptop or desktop PC.
For best learning success, participants should have the background knowledge necessary to follow a course.
We will continue to take applications until notice of application closure is posted.
All applicants also need to check our website for up-to-date information on course availability.
The instructors’ specifications of the prior knowledge expected of participants appear in the course descriptions for each session.
There is a linen change once a week but participants are responsible for their own cleaning of the flat, particularly the shared kitchen facilities.

This should include adequate audio and visual equipment in the form of a webcam (built in or detachable), as well as a reliable internet connection to enable full participation in the online sessions over Zoom.

Accommodation Situated in the grounds of Wivenhoe Park, our campus accommodation is situated just a few minutes away from ESS’s academic and administrative buildings, so residents can, for example, easily use their rooms between lectures to prepare a meal or take a coffee break.
Instructors will assume that everyone doing their course has the required background knowledge and reserve the right to exclude a participant who does not have the required background.
Full payment must then be paid by the date provided to you with your invoice.
There are two options for payment: online payment or bank transfer.

• Participants who withdraw or cancel their enrollment after the 7-day deadline or for reasons other than VISA refusal or medical emergency will not receive any reimbursement.

In order to ensure the fairness of our initiative, to attract people from various backgrounds and to include their voices, we provide a limited number of scholarships to participants with a proven need for financial support.
The scholarships are designed to support participants who would not otherwise be able to cover any or all of the costs of Summer School.
We encourage participants to include a detailed description of their situation and other forms of assistance or recognition they receive to support your application.

Read more Close If you know you'll need a visa to come to Mexico and take part in the summer school, please connect directly with the Mexican Embassy or Consulate that covers your country of residence in order to obtain information about the visa application process and the estimated processing time.

You will need to let us know as soon as possible that you will require a visa so that we can send you a letter of invitation to the summer school, signed by Mila, to be included in the documentation to be submitted with your visa application.
I need financial support to take part in the summer school.
The bursary can help the recipient cover various expenses related to his/her participation in the Summer School, such as the cost of the summer school ticket, accommodation, travel.
Eligible participants may come from less developed countries, disadvantaged socioeconomic environments and/or marginalized communities.
Applicants should make sure to include an estimate of their summer school expenses to enable us to assess the needs of each applicant.
To find out if you need a visa and to find out what steps to take to apply for an entry visa into Mexico, click here .

Do I have to pay for accommodation, meals and transportation?
For accommodation and local (and international) transport, you'll need to cover these details yourself.

Skills Covered C/C++, Fortran, Python Target Audience This program is intended for individuals seeking to develop or strengthen their expertise in HPC, including: Master’s and PhD students in computational science, engineering, or related fields who aim to expand their technical competencies Researchers and engineers interested in applying HPC methodologies to scientific and engineering challenges Industry professionals who require up-to-date
Participant selection will be based on the information provided in the application form.
Participants engage with core topics such as parallel programming models, hardware-aware optimization strategies, scalable software design, and efficient deep learning training techniques.
The course combines theoretical lectures with hands-on laboratory sessions and interactive discussions, enabling direct application of the concepts introduced.
Particular attention is given to the motivation letter; therefore, detailed and carefully considered responses are strongly recommended.

The program introduces the principles and practical use of diverse computing architectures, with particular attention to the combined use of CPUs and GPUs to achieve improved computational performance and efficiency.

In addition, fundamental and applied aspects of deep learning are addressed, including model design, training workflows, and strategies for scaling from single-GPU environments to distributed systems.
g from single-GPU environments to distributed systems.

Participants The SOLAS Summer School is open to an international cohort of about 60 early career researchers.
We are committed to assembling a truly diverse and inclusive cohort, welcoming participants from various geographic regions, backgrounds, abilities and identities.

Objectives The SOLAS Summer School aims to provide a holistic understanding of ocean-atmosphere sciences, equipping participants with the transdisciplinary skills needed to address pressing climate and environmental challenges.
The programme is primarily designed for PhD students and early-stage postdoctoral researchers, but open for applicants with different career paths.
This is an Ocean Decade Endorsed Activity General Information Read more Application and Registration Fees The call for applications is closed.
Read more Contact and Organisers The Summer School 2026 is organised by an international team of researchers and the IPO.

Participants will explore the complex interactions between the ocean, atmosphere, and climate through a dynamic blend of lectures, hands-on practicals, collaborative student-led projects, all aligned with the "SOLAS Science Towards Solutions" SOLAS 3.0 framework.
Our goal is to empower you to translate fundamental knowledge into solutions across the science-policy nexus and thrive in a collaborative, international and diverse research environment.
Student backgrounds will represent the full breadth of SOLAS sciences, from fundamental ocean-atmosphere research to applied solution oriented research with direct societal relevance.
Read more Contact Please send an email to solasschool@xmu.edu.cn if you have any questions or need further assistance regarding the Summer School.
The SOLAS international research network is built on interdisciplinary synergy and a deep commitment to fostering the next generation of scientists.
Building on a legacy of training over 600 early career scientists since 2003, the SOLAS Summer School provides a unique opportunity to learn from world-leading experts in ocean-atmosphere sciences.

evidence_snippets.text: ﻿ OPLSS 2026 | University of Oregon One Stop Apply Visit Give June 22-July 4, 2026 Types, Proofs, and Program Logics Topics Schedule Rates Registration Logistics Archives Policies Sponsors For 24 years, the Oregon Programming Languages Summer School (OPLSS) has focused on teaching the principles of programming languages to students and professionals.

Although the topics change from year to year, a common theme is the significance of fundamental theory in the design and implementation of programming languages, the development of program verification tools, and the application of advanced programming languages.
The summer school attracts participants from around the world and often subsidizes the participation of qualified attendees with limited resources.
Since its inception in 2002, over a thousand participants have attended OPLSS.
The program lasts two weeks and allows ample time for both group and independent study, as well as opportunities to enjoy the many recreational activities available around Eugene.
Recordings of the sessions are available online.

items.page_title: 7th ICTP Summer School on Theory, Mechanisms and Hierarchical Modelling of Climate Dynamics | www.clivar.org

evidence_snippets.text: 29 June - 10 July, 2026 Applications are open The School will introduce participants, with an emphasis on attendees from

29 June - 10 July, 2026 Applications are open The School will introduce participants, with an emphasis on attendees from the Global South, to the GO-SHIP program, inverse models, international programs for observing systems, reanalysis products, and will stimulate their scientific involvement within existing programs.
The School will provide participants with the necessary theoretical and practical knowledge in order to use, manipulate and analyze oceanographic data.
More info here: https://indico.ictp.it/event/11150/ Deadline for applications: 30/04/2026 Topics: Inverse models Observational time series Ocean models and reanalysis Speakers: D.

The School, organized over a 10-day period as an in-person event, will include theoretical lectures on the current state of knowledge as well as practical sessions with the development of student projects to provide a hands-on approach to the understanding of the material presented in the lectures.

The aims are threefold: (i) to use datasets from the Global Ocean Ship-based Hydrographic Investigations Program (GO-SHIP) and inverse modelling techniques to compute oceanic transports; (ii) to analyze existing Atlantic observational arrays (e.g., OSNAP, RAPID, MOVE, SAMBA/SAMOC); (iii) to complement observational products with a variety of ocean reanalysis.

The School, organized over a 10-day period as an in-person event, will include theoretical lectures on the current state of knowledge as well as practical sessions with the development of student projects to provide a hands-on approach to the understanding of the material presented in the lectures.

The aims are threefold: (i) to use datasets from the Global Ocean Ship-based Hydrographic Investigations Program (GO-SHIP) and inverse modelling techniques to compute oceanic transports; (ii) to analyze existing Atlantic observational arrays (e.g., OSNAP, RAPID, MOVE, SAMBA/SAMOC); (iii) to complement observational products with a variety of ocean reanalysis.
